headline: Daredevil Evel Knievel Dies, 2007
body: On November 30, 2007, Robert Craig "Evel" Knievel, the iconic American daredevil and entertainer, passed away at the age of 69. Born in Butte, Montana, Knievel became a global sensation for his death-defying motorcycle stunts, captivating audiences with his fearless attempts to jump over buses, cars, and even the Snake River Canyon. His career, marked by spectacular successes and dramatic crashes, embodied a spirit of audacious risk-taking and showmanship that resonated deeply with the American public. Knievel's Montana roots remained a significant part of his identity, and he was buried in his hometown of Butte. His legacy as the ultimate showman and a symbol of daring continues to inspire and entertain, cementing his place in both Montana and American cultural history.
